Name: Theme
Definition: Direct outcomes of thematic analysis representing recurring patterns of meaning related to the research question.

Name: Taxonomy
Definition: Hierarchical structures mapping the relationships between themes and Subs.

Name: Definition
Definition: Textual definitions or descriptions explaining specific themes.

Name: Example
Definition: Concrete data examples used for detailed explanations of themes.

Name: Source
Definition: Data origins specifying where specific themes came from.

Name: Other theme
Definition: Thematic outcomes that cannot be categorized within standard boundaries.

Name: Concept
Definition: Higher-level abstract ideas derived by further refining the initial themes.

Name: Model/FMK
Definition: Novel conceptual structures or models that emerged from thematic analysis.

Name: Design Insight
Definition: Actionable insights, such as design implications, intended for future design.

Name: Example
Definition: Concrete examples illustrating the application of a model, framework, or design insight.

Name: Quantitatives
Definition: Numerical data metrics directly related to or measuring the themes.

Name: Frequency
Definition: Quantitative counts representing the frequencies of each theme.

Name: Obj-measured
Definition: Objectively-measured quantitative values (e.g., time, accuracy).

Name: Self-reported
Definition: Subjective, self-reported quantitative values (e.g., SUS, NASA-TLX scores).

Name: Grid
Definition: Structured visual representations organizing data into a grid of rows and columns.

Name: Table
Definition: Text-centric grid layouts designed for detailed reading where cells contain alphanumeric data.

Name: Matrix
Definition: Visual-centric grid layouts designed for scanning relationships, where cells contain discrete visual marks.

Name: Heatmap
Definition: Value-centric grid layouts utilizing color intensity to visually encode magnitude, frequency, or density.

Name: Diagram
Definition: Schematic representations illustrating relationships between conceptual entities.

Name: Block diagram
Definition: Diagrams laying out information in schematic blocks without explicit interconnections to show grouping and categorization.

Name: Network diagram
Definition: Diagrams illustrating relationships by explicitly connecting blocks with lines.

Name: Euler diagram
Definition: Diagrams using spatial areas to represent relationships, such as Venn diagrams and Onion diagrams.

Name: Other diagram
Definition: Any diagram variants that do not fit the primary classifications.

Name: Image
Definition: Visual representations capturing a specific scene or concept visually.

Name: Photo
Definition: Images that capture real-world scenes.

Name: Screenshot
Definition: Images that depict digital content captured directly from screens.

Name: Sketch
Definition: Images consisting of hand-drawn or hand-written rough drawings and texts.

Name: Illustration
Definition: Images consisting of polished graphics generated via design tools.

Name: Chart
Definition: Graphics mapping quantitative data to distinct visual elements.

Name: Bar chart
Definition: Charts representing quantitative values primarily through bar heights.

Name: Grouped bar
Definition: A subcategory of bar charts that groups multiple bars together for comparison.

Name: Stacked bar
Definition: A subcategory of bar charts that stacks bars vertically to show part-to-whole relationships.

Name: Range symbol
Definition: Charts incorporating visual indicators for errors or confidence intervals.

Name: Box plot
Definition: Charts used to demonstrate statistically valid differences in scores or distributions.

Name: Other chart
Definition: Charts with low appearance rates that are not subcategorized (e.g., line charts, scatterplots).

Name: Msc. Vis
Definition: Miscellaneous visual representations that do not fit into the primary visual encoding taxonomies.

Name: Msc. Vis
Definition: Miscellaneous visual representations that do not fit into the primary visual encoding taxonomies.
